# Questions in Livestream 260

Live Stream #260: Forty-Two Lost Questions

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=678" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:18</a> | On the plagues of Egypt, why did everyone have to stay indoors on Passover night, and did the mixed multitude's firstborn die? | He says the mixed multitude was not excluded; their firstborn would die if they did not observe Passover, and staying indoors also avoided chaos in the streets. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=820" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:40</a> | Was Egypt a military dictatorship during the period of British occupation? | Yes. He says it was a military dictatorship from the Egyptian perspective because the British occupying force ruled while Egyptians did not get to vote. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=939" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:39</a> | Why did Christians start using codexes, like normal books, instead of scrolls? | He says codexes were much more convenient than scrolls, especially for looking things up, and the Christian era coincided with the codex becoming common. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=1175" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:35</a> | Roughly what share of extant Hebrew literature from its period does the Tanakh comprise? | He says he cannot answer because he does not know the full extent of Second Temple period literature. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=1252" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:52</a> | Given the prevalence of harems in ancient Egypt, why were succession crises and dynasty changes so common? | He says Egypt had relatively few dynasties over 3,000 years, and succession crises could result from heirs dying, shifts in power, and invasions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=1703" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:23</a> | What do you think about classes that claim they can teach good writing? | He says quality varies widely and rejects any promise that someone can be made a good writer in a weekend. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=1959" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:39</a> | Do you have any comment on Deep Fat Simple's plague video, or is it worthy of a popcorn review? | He does not think it deserves a popcorn review, but he also does not recommend it because he says it is AI-generated and hallucinates Egyptian gods. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=2267" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:47</a> | Why do early-date proponents need Amenhotep II to be the Exodus pharaoh given the high chronology problem? | He says it is mostly tradition, tied to a traditional requirement that the Exodus pharaoh's predecessor reign 40 years. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=2338" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:58</a> | Is Codex H the earliest manuscript to include chapter divisions for the Bible? | No. He says earlier codices also have divisions, though such divisions were not standardized. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=2566" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:46</a> | What is Pierre's accent? | He says Pierre is Parisian French, not Quebecois. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=2670" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:30</a> | What is your reaction to Alberta getting enough signatures to trigger a referendum on leaving Canada? | He jokes that he would like to move to Alberta before it happens, and says the federal system is not working well. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=3159" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:39</a> | Where should people buy your books so you benefit the most? | He says it mostly does not matter because publishers set the discount structure across retailers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=3392" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:32</a> | Do the candlestick churches in Revelation 1:12-20 still apply to the church today? | He says the passage is too large to cover briefly; in some ways it still applies as principles or church types, while also referring to actual churches in Asia Minor. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=3501" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">58:21</a> | Did kings in Egypt or Thailand tend to have more wives? | He cannot compare them directly, but says Egyptian kings commonly had very large harems, often around 50 to 100 wives. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=3543" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:03</a> | How severe could royal fertility problems be? | He says the problems ranged from difficulty conceiving to complete infertility, with causes including inbreeding and health factors. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=3598" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:58</a> | How mean were you as reviewer number two? | He says he was honest, not nasty, and jokes that if he wanted to be mean he would have recommended publishing the article as-is. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=3853" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:04:13</a> | What is your favorite brand of noodles? | He says Sapporo Ichiban is the closest answer for ramen, though he prefers Italian noodles for day-to-day eating. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=4619" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:59</a> | How did you come to know Christ? | He says it was uneventful: at age seven, after hearing the gospel at a Royal Heirs concert, he accepted Christ in the back of the car on the way home. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=4910" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:50</a> | Do you recommend a reputable English translation of the Ethiopian canon of the Bible? | He says he does not have one and recommends asking an Ethiopian church because they would know that tradition better. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=5060" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:20</a> | Are there practical uses for cursive writing without writing a book? | He treats writing as a muscle and recommends starting with a notebook and writing every day. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=5558" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:38</a> | What are the main objections people have to the biblical Exodus, and why do they hold them? | He says the main objection is the claim that there is no evidence, and he ties the deeper reason to whether people trust biblical sources. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=5678" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:38</a> | How many times does someone who has been in many churches need to be rebaptized? | He says changing churches normally does not require rebaptism, though some churches may require it and leaving a non-Christian cult may be different. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=6591" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:51</a> | Does Amazon pay American or Canadian tax on the share it takes from book sales? | He says that depends on the retail side being used, such as `amazon.ca` versus `amazon.com`. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=7048" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:28</a> | Do you have thoughts on early human diet and what it means for modern diets? | He says we are still not sure enough about Homo sapiens diet to treat it as a simple guide for what people should eat now. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=7814" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:14</a> | What fish are in the aquarium? | He says there are no fish, mostly cherry shrimp, plus a couple of nerite snails and plants. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=8327" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:47</a> | How does monarchial Trinitarianism differ from other models of the Trinity? | He describes it as three coequal, coeternal persons organized as Father, Son, and Holy Spirit, and contrasts it with subordinationist, social, eternal-generation, and qualitative models. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=8953" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:13</a> | Will Christians learn about the Trinity in heaven? | Yes. He says believers will continue exploring that mystery eternally, even without exhausting it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=8985" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:45</a> | When will your article on the genealogies of Genesis be published? | He says it will eventually be published when he has time to finish it, but he has many writing commitments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=9211" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:31</a> | Do you intend to publish the Berlin pedestal paper in another journal? | Yes. He says he will eventually publish it and has already dusted it off for another look. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=9243" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:03</a> | Is it a problem to keep research up to date because publication takes so long? | He says it can be for time-sensitive work, but most of his work is not time-sensitive, and peer reviewers often point out new research to add. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=9321" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:21</a> | What should someone do with a gap year between undergraduate and graduate study? | He says to keep studying and use the time intentionally rather than drifting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=9987" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:27</a> | Do Christians and Jews have a divine right to the land of Israel? | He says Christians do not, and he ties the biblical land promise specifically to the descendants of Jacob. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=10071" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:51</a> | How are demons and aliens different from each other? | He says demons are part of a known spiritual category in his framework, whereas alien life remains unproven and speculative. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=10215" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:15</a> | Could some unknown aerial sightings also be angels? | He says yes, it is possible, but an unexplained sighting by itself does not prove whether it is angelic, demonic, alien, or something else. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=10471" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:54:31</a> | How do UFOs seem to break the laws of physics? | He says we do not really know that they do break physics, only that the reports are difficult to explain with current assumptions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=10833" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:00:33</a> | What is the relationship between the physical world and the spiritual world, and do they influence each other? | He says he is not sure how they interact, only that they do, and notes that even the physical world is hard to define at a fundamental level. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=11336" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:56</a> | Did Egyptians, Romans, and other ancient nations have UFO sightings or something similar? | He says ancient people did see unexplained phenomena, but they interpreted them through categories such as omens rather than modern UFO language. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=11402" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:02</a> | Why does Exodus prescribe stoning the ox instead of cutting its throat, and how does one stone an ox? | He says stoning prevents anyone from profiting from the meat of a dangerous animal that killed someone; the method is essentially throwing large sharp stones. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=11687" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:14:47</a> | What does "our body is a temple" have to do with rituals, laws, and sacrifice? | He calls the comparison a category error: the body as temple concerns living unto Christ with the Spirit in us, while ritual law and sacrifice were fulfilled in Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=12228" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:48</a> | Is it sinful to smoke? | He says smoking itself is not usually sinful, but it is foolish and can become sinful when it harms others or misuses money needed by the family. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=12551" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:11</a> | Who is the person devoted to herem in Leviticus 27, and is Leviticus 27:29 proof that Israelites practiced human sacrifice? | He reads the passage in the context of vows and argues against using it as proof of approved Israelite human sacrifice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=13043" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:37:23</a> | Do you think the slaves in the biography of Ahmose are Israelites? | Yes. He thinks many likely were Israelites because Avaris was in Goshen, though he also says other people lived there too. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=13155" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:39:15</a> | Why do ancient Jewish and early Christian interpretations either deny Jephthah sacrificed his daughter or criticize the sacrifice as immoral? | He says the sacrifice was immoral and the text is about not making rash vows; Jephthah had ways out but did not take them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=14047" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:54:07</a> | What is the best movie portrayal of Jesus? | He says his favorite is *The Gospel of John* because it is very true to the Gospel it portrays. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=14454" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:00:54</a> | Why did Jesus call the Canaanite woman a dog, or have we misconstrued the meaning of his response? | He says Jesus was using the disciples' own pejorative language to teach them that salvation is for Gentiles as well as Jews. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=14480" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:01:20</a> | What Old Testament movies do you recommend besides *The Ten Commandments*? | He still treats *The Ten Commandments* as the best Exodus film and does not sound very impressed with most alternatives. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/OcfgbRnZYrI?t=15214" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:13:34</a> | To refute the pyramids-Orion conspiracy, can they dig where Sirius is supposed to be? | He rejects the Orion theory as recent and unsupported, saying the proposed alignment is impractical and has no ancient evidence behind it. |
